title: alcea biennis subsp. cretica
intro: Alcea biennis subsp. cretica, also known as the Great Harricot or Balkan Hollyhock, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the mallow family (Malvaceae). Alcea biennis subsp. cretica has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat various ailments such as digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions due to its high content of flavonoids and saponins. Herbal preparations of Alcea biennis subsp. cretica include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures that can be used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
